notes: Primary key; scale is 0 so INT64 is safe and avoids unnecessary NUMERIC overhead. First component of the merge key (employee_id + updated_date) in sp_mysqltobq_load.sql. Post-load uniqueness check must be enforced on this column.
PII field. BigQuery STRING supersedes MySQL varchar; no length constraint is enforced natively in BigQuery — source length limit (50) should be documented in schema validation rules.
PII field. BigQuery STRING supersedes MySQL varchar; source length limit (50) should be documented in schema validation rules.
PII field; NOT NULL in source. Apply BigQuery column-level security policy tags to restrict access to PII-authorised principals.
PII field; nullable in source and target. Apply BigQuery column-level security policy tags. Pre-load null validation should permit NULLs for this column.
Direct DATE-to-DATE mapping; no timezone conversion required. MySQL DATE has no time component.
Third clustering column; optimises queries filtering or grouping by job role. Source length limit (20) should be captured in schema validation rules.
BigQuery NUMERIC (29 significant digits, 9 decimal places) safely accommodates decimal(8,2). Sensitive compensation data — access controls should be reviewed as part of PII governance.
Nullable in source and target; NULL semantically means no commission applicable. Pre-load validation must permit NULLs for this column.

Self-referential FK to employee_id; nullable for root-level managers. Scale is 0 so INT64 is safe. Referential integrity is not enforced natively in BigQuery — integrity checks should be implemented in the validation layer.
First clustering column; high-selectivity dimension key that prunes data scanned for department-scoped analytical queries. Nullable in source; BigQuery does not enforce FK constraints.

Second clustering column; low-cardinality field (e.g. ACTIVE, INACTIVE) that significantly reduces bytes scanned when queries filter on employment status. Source length limit (10) should be documented in schema validation rules.
Source system record creation timestamp. MySQL TIMESTAMP is stored and retrieved in UTC; BigQuery TIMESTAMP is UTC-normalised — no timezone conversion needed. NOT NULL in source.

Partition column with DAY granularity; enables partition pruning for time-bounded incremental queries and supports the 60-day retention policy enforcement via BigQuery partition expiry. Second component of the sp_mysqltobq_load.sql merge key (employee_id + updated_date). Nullable in source — NULL rows land in the BigQuery __NULL__ partition; monitor for unexpected NULLs in post-load validation.
